title: Cryptographic Randomness, Entropy, Keys, Salts, and Nonces · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible randomness and uniqueness contract with one primitive, parameter, nonce, context, key, parser, trust, timing, migration, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in randomness and uniqueness contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Use current Go cryptographic randomness APIs for keys, tokens, salts, nonces, and blinding while handling failure according to the API contract.
Distinguish entropy sources, deterministic random bit generators, unpredictability, uniqueness, bias, and statistical appearance.
Generate algorithm-compatible keys at supported strengths and retain provenance, purpose, owner, and creation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary Cryptography and Go threat-to-evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

For Cryptography and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary, keep Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 5613 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate public unique salts, unpredictable bearer tokens, nonces, IVs, seeds, and secret keys by their exact guarantees.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crypto-go-salt-token-boundary-1q6jyw" that uses case 6739 to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import cryptographic packages, generate or handle production keys, open sockets, contact TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, compliance services, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, standard crypto APIs, authoritative vectors, fuzz, race, native side-channel measurement, interoperability, controlled network and key-custody systems, load, fault, recovery, compliance, and production gates.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary, keep Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary structural check is only one gate. randomness and uniqueness contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug cryptographic engineering team handles case 8328 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate public unique salts, unpredictable bearer tokens, nonces, IVs, seeds, and secret keys by their exact guarantees.

For Cryptography and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Crypto Go Salt Token Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Salt, nonce, IV, token, seed, and key are synonyms.
